title: Defra AI Coding Standards
summary: Rules and skills for consistent AI-assisted development across Defra.
author: Platform Team
whatThisIs: A repository of rules, skills, and agent configurations for AI coding tools used across Defra projects. It covers everything from code style to PR review conventions.
whenToUseThis: Use this when setting up a new Defra project with AI coding assistants. It gives your tools the same standards your team follows.
whatsInside: 12 rules files covering code style, testing patterns, and PR review conventions, plus 3 skills for automated refactoring.

title: User Story Generator
summary: Generates user stories from meeting transcripts.
author: Jane Smith
whatThisPromptDoes: Give it a meeting transcript and it produces a set of user stories in standard format. Each story includes acceptance criteria and a clear definition of done.
whyThisPromptWorks: It uses structured output formatting and chain-of-thought reasoning to break down free-form discussions into actionable stories. The key insight is asking the AI to identify the actors first before writing stories.
howToUseTheResult: Review each story for accuracy against the original transcript, then import them into your backlog tool. They will need sizing by the team.
promptText:
You are a business analyst working on a government digital service.

Read the following meeting transcript and produce user stories in standard format.

For each story include:
- A title
- As a [role], I want [feature], so that [benefit]
- Acceptance criteria (Given/When/Then)
- Definition of done

Transcript:
[DOCUMENT]

Topic: [TOPIC]

title: Sprint Planning Pipeline
summary: End-to-end sprint planning from backlog to task breakdown.
author: Delivery Team
whatThisWorkflowAchieves: Takes a raw backlog and produces a fully planned sprint with sized stories and task breakdowns. You walk away with a sprint ready to start.
whyThisWorkflowMatters: Breaking planning into steps lets you review and adjust at each stage rather than trusting a single massive prompt. Each checkpoint is a chance to catch mistakes before they compound.

steps.title: Prioritise the backlog
steps.context: Start by getting the AI to rank stories by business value and dependencies. This gives you a starting order to work from.
steps.promptText:
Review the following backlog items and rank them by business value, considering dependencies between items.

For each item explain your ranking rationale in one sentence.

[BACKLOG_ITEMS]
steps.whatToDoWithOutput: Check the ranking makes sense and adjust any obvious misplacements before continuing.

steps.title: Size the stories
steps.context: Now estimate each prioritised story using the team velocity as context.
steps.promptText:
Given the following prioritised stories and a team velocity of [VELOCITY] points per sprint, estimate story points for each.

Use the Fibonacci scale (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13). Explain your estimate briefly.

[PRIORITISED_STORIES]
steps.whatToDoWithOutput: Validate estimates against your team knowledge and adjust where the AI has over or underestimated.
